examples:
on block fertilize:
	send "Fertilized %size of fertilized blocks% blocks got fertilized."
EvtBlockFertilize.name: On Block Fertilize
EvtBlockFertilize.description: Called when a player fertilizes blocks.

examples:
on entity potion effect modification:
	broadcast "A potion effect was added to %event-entity%!"
on entity potion effect of night vision added:
	message "You can now see in the dark!"
on entity potion effect of strength removed:
	message "You're now weaker!"
EvtEntityPotionEffect.name: On Entity Potion Effect
EvtEntityPotionEffect.description: Called when an entity's potion effect is modified.

examples:
on chat:
	if the player has permission "owner":
		set the chat format to "<red>[player]<light gray>: <light red>[message]"
	else if the player has permission "admin":
		set the chat format to "<light red>[player]<light gray>: <orange>[message]"
	else: # default message format
		set the chat format to "<orange>[player]<light gray>: <white>[message]"
EvtChat.name: On Chat
EvtChat.description: Called whenever a player chats. Use chat format to change message format. Use chat recipients to edit chat recipients.

examples:
on item despawn of diamond:
	send "Not my precious!"
	cancel event
EvtItemDespawn.name: On Item Despawn
EvtItemDespawn.description: Called when an item is about to be despawned from the world, usually 5 minutes after it was dropped.

examples:
on beacon toggle:
on beacon activate:
on beacon deactivate:
EvtBeaconToggle.name: On Beacon Toggle
EvtBeaconToggle.description: Called when a beacon is activated or deactivated.

examples:
on place:
on place of a furnace, crafting table or chest:
on break of chest[type=right] or chest[type=left]
EvtPlace.name: On Place
EvtPlace.description: Called when a player places a block.

examples: on kick:
EvtKick.name: On Kick
EvtKick.description: Called when a player is kicked from the server. You can change the kick message or cancel the event entirely.

examples:
on piglin barter:
	if barter drops contain diamond:
		send "Diamonds belong in the money pit!" to player
		cancel event
EvtPiglinBarter.name: On Piglin Barter
EvtPiglinBarter.description:
Called when a piglin finishes bartering. A piglin may start bartering after picking up an item on its bartering list.
Cancelling will prevent piglins from dropping items, but will still make them pick up the input.

examples:
on firework explode:
	if event-colors contains red:
on firework exploding colored red, light green and black:
on firework explosion colored rgb 0, 255, 0:
	broadcast "A firework colored %colors% was exploded at %location%!"
EvtFireworkExplode.name: On Firework Explode

examples:
on world save of "world":
	broadcast "The world %event-world% has been saved"
EvtWorldSave.name: On World Save
EvtWorldSave.description: Called when a world is saved to disk. Usually all worlds are saved simultaneously, but world management plugins could change this.

examples:
on worldborder center change:
	broadcast "The center of %event-worldborder% has moved from %past event-location% to %event-location%"
EvtWorldBorderCenterChange.name: On World Border Center Change
EvtWorldBorderCenterChange.description:
Called when a world border's center has changed.
This event does not get called for virtual borders.

examples:
on inventory open:
	close player's inventory
EvtInventoryOpen.name: On Inventory Open
EvtInventoryOpen.description: Called when an inventory is opened for player.

examples:
on join:
	message "Welcome on our awesome server!"
	broadcast "%player% just joined the server!"
EvtJoin.name: On Join
EvtJoin.description: Called when the player joins the server. The player is already in a world when this event is called, so if you want to prevent players from joining you should prefer on connect over this event.

examples:
on item mend:
	chance of 50%:
		cancel the event
		send "Oops! Mending failed!" to player
EvtItemMend.name: On Item Mend
EvtItemMend.description: Called when a player has an item repaired via the Mending enchantment.

examples:
on love mode enter:
	cancel event # No one is allowed love here
EvtLoveModeEnter.name: On Love Mode Enter
EvtLoveModeEnter.description: Called whenever an entity enters a state of being in love.

examples:
on player world change:
	world is "city"
	send "Welcome to the City!"
EvtPlayerWorldChange.name: On Player World Change
EvtPlayerWorldChange.description: Called when a player enters a world. Does not work with other entities!

examples: on zombie breaking a wood door:
EvtZombieBreakDoor.name: On Zombie Break Door
EvtZombieBreakDoor.description: Called when a zombie is done breaking a wooden door. Can be cancelled to prevent the zombie from breaking the door.

examples:
on swim toggle:
	event-entity does not have permission "swim"
	cancel event
EvtSwimToggle.name: On Swim Toggle
EvtSwimToggle.description: Called when an entity swims or stops swimming.

examples:
on block drop:
	broadcast event-player
	broadcast past event-block
	broadcast event-block
	broadcast event-items
	broadcast event-entities
on block drop of oak log:
EvtBlockDrop.name: On Block Drop
EvtBlockDrop.description:
Called when a block broken by a player drops something.
  • event-player: The player that broke the block
  • past event-block: The block that was broken
  • event-block: The block after being broken
  • event-items (or drops): The drops of the block
  • event-entities: The entities of the dropped items
If the breaking of the block leads to others being broken, such as torches, they will appearin "event-items" and "event-entities".

examples:
on player shoot bow:
	chance of 30%:
		damage event-slot by 10
		send "Your bow has taken increased damage!" to shooter

on stray shooting bow:
	set {_e} to event-entity
	spawn a cow at {_e}:
		set velocity of entity to velocity of {_e}
	set event-entity to last spawned entity
EvtEntityShootBow.name: On Entity Shoot Bow
EvtEntityShootBow.description:
Called when an entity shoots a bow.
event-entity refers to the shot projectile/entity.

examples:
on portal:
	broadcast "%player% has entered a portal!"
on player portal:
	player's world is world("wilderness")
	set world of event-location to player's world
	add 9000 to x-pos of event-location
on entity portal:
	broadcast "A %type of event-entity% has entered a portal!
EvtPortal.name: On Portal
EvtPortal.description: Called when a player or an entity uses a nether or end portal. Note that 'on entity portal' event does not apply to players. Cancel the event to prevent the entity from teleporting.

examples:
on a zombie transforming due to curing:
on mooshroom transforming:
on zombie, skeleton or slime transform:
EvtEntityTransform.name: On Entity Transform
EvtEntityTransform.description:
Called when an entity is about to be replaced by another entity.
Examples when it's called include; when a zombie gets cured and a villager spawns, an entity drowns in water like a zombie that turns to a drown, an entity that gets frozen in powder snow, a mooshroom that when sheared, spawns a new cow.

examples: on vehicle create:
EvtVehicleCreate.name: On Vehicle Create
EvtVehicleCreate.description: Called when a new vehicle is created, e.g. when a player places a boat or minecart.

examples:
on breeding of llamas:
	send "When a %breeding mother% and %breeding father% love each other very much they make %offspring%" to breeder
EvtEntityBreed.name: On Entity Breed
EvtEntityBreed.description: Called whenever two animals begin to conceive a child. The type can be specified.

examples: on inventory pickup:
EvtInventoryPickup.name: On Inventory Pickup
EvtInventoryPickup.description: Called when an inventory (a hopper, a hopper minecart, etc.) picks up an item

examples:
on bell ring:
	send "<gold>Ding-dong!<reset>" to all players in radius 10 of event-block
EvtBellRing.name: On Bell Ring
EvtBellRing.description: Called when a bell is rung.

examples:
on inventory item move:
	broadcast "%holder of past event-inventory% is transporting %event-item% to %holder of event-inventory%!"
EvtInventoryItemMove.name: On Inventory Item Move
EvtInventoryItemMove.description:
Called when an entity or block (e.g. hopper) tries to move items directly from one inventory to another.
When this event is called, the initiator may have already removed the item from the source inventory and is ready to move it into the destination inventory.
If this event is cancelled, the items will be returned to the source inventory.

examples:
on beacon effect:
	broadcast applied effect
	broadcast event-player
	broadcast event-block
on primary beacon effect apply of haste:
on application of secondary beacon effect:
on beacon effect of speed:
EvtBeaconEffect.name: On Beacon Effect
EvtBeaconEffect.description: Called when a player gets an effect from a beacon.

examples:
on vehicle enter:
	entity is a player
	cancel event
EvtVehicleEnter.name: On Vehicle Enter
EvtVehicleEnter.description: Called when an entity enters a vehicle, either deliberately (players) or by falling into them (mobs).

examples:
on walking on dirt or grass:
on stepping on stone:
EvtMoveOn.name: On Move On
EvtMoveOn.description:
Called when a player moves onto a certain type of block.
Please note that using this event can cause lag if there are many players online.

examples:
# Stops endermen from getting angry players with the permission "safeFrom.enderman"
on enderman enrage:
	if player has permission "safeFrom.enderman":
		cancel event
EvtEndermanEnrage.name: On Enderman Enrage
EvtEndermanEnrage.description:
Called when an enderman gets mad because a player looked at them.
Note: This does not stop enderman from targeting the player as a result of getting damaged.

examples:
on piston extend:
	broadcast "A piston is extending!"
EvtPistonExtend.name: On Piston Extend
EvtPistonExtend.description: Called when a piston is about to extend.

EvtFlow.name: On Flow
EvtFlow.description: Called when a blocks flows or teleports to another block. This not only applies to water and lava, but teleporting dragon eggs as well.

examples:
on projectile collide:
	teleport shooter of event-projectile to event-entity
EvtProjectileCollide.name: On Projectile Collide
EvtProjectileCollide.description: Called when a projectile collides with an entity.

examples:
on jump:
	event-player does not have permission "jump"
	cancel event
EvtJump.name: On Jump
EvtJump.description:
Called whenever a player jumps.
This event requires PaperSpigot.

examples:
on anvil damage:
	cancel the event
EvtAnvilDamage.name: On Anvil Damage
EvtAnvilDamage.description:
Called when an anvil is damaged/broken from being used to repair/rename items.
Note: this does not include anvil damage from falling.

examples: on player start spectating of a zombie:
EvtSpectate.name: On Spectate
EvtSpectate.description: Called with a player starts, stops or swaps spectating an entity.

examples: on player filling a bucket:
EvtBucketFill.name: On Bucket fill
EvtBucketFill.description: Called when a player fills a bucket.

examples:
on sign change:
	line 2 is empty
	set line 1 to "<red>%line 1%"
EvtSignChange.name: On Sign Change
EvtSignChange.description: As signs are placed empty, this event is called when a player is done editing a sign.

examples: on spread:
EvtSpread.name: On Spread
EvtSpread.description: Called when a new block forms as a result of a block that can spread, e.g. water or mushrooms.

examples: on combust:
EvtCombust.name: On Combust
EvtCombust.description: Called when an entity is set on fire, e.g. by fire or lava, a fireball, or by standing in direct sunlight (zombies, skeletons).

examples:
on mine:
on break of stone:
on break of chest[facing=north]:
on break of potatoes[age=7]:
EvtBreakMine.name: On Break / Mine
EvtBreakMine.description: Called when a block is broken by a player. If you use 'on mine', only events where the broken block dropped something will call the trigger.

examples:
on sponge absorb:
	loop absorbed blocks:
		broadcast "%loop-block% was absorbed by a sponge"!
EvtSpongeAbsorb.name: On Sponge Absorb
EvtSpongeAbsorb.description: Called when a sponge absorbs blocks.

examples:
on sprint toggle:
	player is not sprinting
	send "Run!"
EvtSprintToggle.name: On Sprint Toggle
EvtSprintToggle.description: Called when a player starts or stops sprinting. Use is sprinting to get whether the player was sprinting before the event was called.

examples:
on player ready arrow:
	selected bow's name is "Spectral Bow"
	if selected arrow is not a spectral arrow:
		cancel event
EvtReadyArrow.name: On Ready Arrow
EvtReadyArrow.description:
Called when a player is firing a bow and the server is choosing an arrow to use.
Cancelling this event will skip the current arrow item and fire a new event for the next arrow item.
The arrow and bow in the event can be accessed with the Readied Arrow/Bow expression.

examples:
on swap hand items:
	event-player's tool is a diamond sword
	cancel event
EvtHandItemSwap.name: On Hand Item Swap
EvtHandItemSwap.description:
Called whenever a player swaps the items in their main- and offhand slots.
Works also when one or both of the slots are empty.
The event is called before the items are actually swapped,
so when you use the player's tool or player's offtool expressions,
they will return the values before the swap -
this enables you to cancel the event before anything happens.

examples:
on smelt:
	clear the smelted item
on smelt of raw iron:
	broadcast smelted item
	set the smelted item to iron block
EvtSmelt.name: On Smelt
EvtSmelt.description: Called when a furnace smelts an item in its input slot.

examples:
on grow:
on grow of tree:
on grow of wheat[age=7]:
on grow from a sapling:
on grow into tree:
on grow from a sapling into tree:
on grow of wheat, carrots, or potatoes:
on grow into tree, giant mushroom, cactus:
on grow from wheat[age=0] to wheat[age=1] or wheat[age=2]:
EvtGrow.name: On Grow
EvtGrow.description:
Called when a tree, giant mushroom or plant grows to next stage.
"of" matches any grow event, "from" matches only the old state, "into" matches only the new state,and "from into" requires matching both the old and new states.
Using "and" lists in this event is equivalent to using "or" lists. The event will trigger if any one of the elements is what grew.

examples:
on resurrect attempt:
	entity is player
	entity has permission "admin.undying"
	uncancel the event
EvtResurrectAttempt.name: On Resurrect Attempt
EvtResurrectAttempt.description: Called when an entity dies, always. If they are not holding a totem, this is cancelled - you can, however, uncancel it.

EvtShoot.name: On Shoot
EvtShoot.description: Called whenever a projectile is shot. Use the shooter expression to get who shot the projectile.

examples:
on first join:
	broadcast "Welcome %player% to the server!"
EvtFirstJoin.name: On First Join
EvtFirstJoin.description: Called when a player joins the server for the first time.

examples: on chunk load:
EvtChunkLoad.name: On Chunk Load
EvtChunkLoad.description: Called when a chunk loads. The chunk might or might not contain mobs when it's loaded.

examples:
on sheep grow wool:
	cancel event
EvtSheepRegrowWool.name: On Sheep Regrow Wool
EvtSheepRegrowWool.description: Called when sheep regrows its sheared wool back.

examples:
on player experience cooldown change:
	broadcast event-player
	broadcast event-timespan
	broadcast past event-timespan
	broadcast xp cooldown change reason
EvtExperienceCooldownChange.name: On Experience Cooldown Change
EvtExperienceCooldownChange.description:
Called when a player's experience cooldown changes.
Experience cooldown is how long until a player can pick up another orb of experience.

examples:
on world unload:
	broadcast "the %event-world% has been unloaded!"
EvtWorldUnload.name: On World Unload
EvtWorldUnload.description: Called when a world is unloaded. This event will never be called if you don't have a world management plugin.

EvtProjectileHit.name: On Projectile Hit
EvtProjectileHit.description: Called when a projectile hits an entity or a block.

examples:
on toggling gliding:
	cancel the event # bad idea, but you CAN do it!
EvtGlidingStateChange.name: On Gliding State Change
EvtGlidingStateChange.description: Called when an entity toggles glider on or off, or when server toggles gliding state of an entity forcibly.

examples:
on player block harvest:
	send "You have harvested %event-block% that dropped %event-items% using your %item of event-slot% in your %event-equipment slot%"

on crop harvesting of sweet berry bush:
	chance 5%:
		set drops to a diamond
	chance 1%
		cancel the drops
EvtHarvestBlock.name: On Harvest Block
EvtHarvestBlock.description:
Called when a player harvests a block.
A block being harvested is when a block drops items and the state of the block is changed, but the block is not broken.
An example is harvesting berries from a berry bush.

EvtFlightToggle.name: On Flight Toggle
EvtFlightToggle.description: Called when a players stops/starts flying.

examples: on throw of an egg:
EvtEggThrow.name: On Egg Throw
EvtEggThrow.description: Called when a player throws an egg and it lands. You can just use the shoot event in most cases. However, this event allows modification of properties like the hatched entity type and the number of entities to hatch.

examples:
on player leash of a sheep:
	send "Baaaaa--" to player
on player leash:
	send "<%event-entity%> Let me go!" to player
on unleash:
	broadcast "<%event-entity%> I'm free"
on player unleash:
	send "<%event-entity%> Thanks for freeing me!" to player
EvtLeashUnleash.name: On Leash / Unleash
EvtLeashUnleash.description: Called when an entity is leashed or unleashed. Cancelling these events will prevent the leashing or unleashing from occurring.

examples:
on heal:
on player healing from a regeneration potion:
on healing of a zombie, cow or a wither:
	heal reason is healing potion
	cancel event
EvtHeal.name: On Heal
EvtHeal.description: Called when an entity is healed, e.g. by eating (players), being fed (pets), or by the effect of a potion of healing (overworld mobs) or harm (nether mobs).

examples: on book edit:
EvtBookEdit.name: On Book Edit
EvtBookEdit.description: Called when a player edits a book.

examples:
on piston retract:
	broadcast "A piston is retracting!"
EvtPistonRetract.name: On Piston Retract
EvtPistonRetract.description: Called when a piston is about to retract.

examples: on preparing craft of torch:
EvtPrepareCraft.name: On Prepare Craft
EvtPrepareCraft.description: Called just before displaying crafting result to player. Note that setting the result item might or might not work due to Bukkit bugs.

examples:
on riptide:
	send "You are riptiding!"
EvtRiptide.name: On Riptide
EvtRiptide.description:
Called when the player activates the riptide enchantment, using their trident to propel them through the air.
Note: the riptide action is performed client side, so manipulating the player in this event may have undesired effects.

examples:
on worldborder bounds change:
	broadcast "The diameter of %event-worldborder% is changing from %past event-number% to %event-number% over the next %event-timespan%"
EvtWorldBorderBoundsChange.name: On World Border Bounds Change
EvtWorldBorderBoundsChange.description:
Called when a world border changes its bounds, either over time, or instantly.
This event does not get called for virtual borders.

examples:
# prevents sand from falling
on block physics:
	block is sand
	cancel event
EvtPhysics.name: On Physics
EvtPhysics.description: Called when a physics check is done on a block. By cancelling this event you can prevent some things from happening, e.g. sand falling, dirt turning into grass, torches dropping if their supporting block is destroyed, etc.Please note that using this event might cause quite some lag since it gets called extremely often.

EvtInventorySlotChange.name: On Inventory Slot Change
EvtInventorySlotChange.description:
Called when a slot in a player's inventory is changed.
Warning: setting the event-slot to a new item can result in an infinite loop.

examples:
on player enters a chunk:
	send "You entered a chunk: %past event-chunk% -> %event-chunk%!" to player
EvtPlayerChunkEnter.name: On Player Chunk Enter
EvtPlayerChunkEnter.description: Called when a player enters a chunk. Note that this event is based on 'player move' event, and may be called frequent internally.

EvtInventoryClose.name: On Inventory Close
EvtInventoryClose.description: Called when player's currently viewed inventory is closed.

examples: on bed enter:
EvtBedEnter.name: On Bed Enter
EvtBedEnter.description: Called when a player starts sleeping.

EvtBlockDamage.name: On Block Damage
EvtBlockDamage.description: Called when a player starts to break a block. You can usually just use the leftclick event for this.

examples:
on enchant prepare:
	set enchant offer 1 to sharpness 1
	set the cost of enchant offer 1 to 10 levels
EvtEnchantPrepare.name: On Enchant Prepare
EvtEnchantPrepare.description: Called when a player puts an item into enchantment table. This event may be called multiple times. To get the enchant item, see the enchant item expression

examples: on portal create:
EvtPortalCreate.name: On Portal Create
EvtPortalCreate.description: Called when a portal is created, either by a player or mob lighting an obsidian frame on fire, or by a nether portal creating its teleportation target in the nether/overworld. In Minecraft 1.14+, you can use the player in this event. Please note that there may not always be a player (or other entity) in this event.

examples: on craft:
EvtCraft.name: On Craft
EvtCraft.description: Called when a player crafts an item.

examples: on step on pressure plate:
EvtPressurePlateTrip.name: On Pressure Plate / Trip
EvtPressurePlateTrip.description: Called when a player steps on a pressure plate or tripwire respectively.

examples:
on input key press:
	send "You are pressing: %event-inputkeys%" to player
EvtPlayerInput.name: On Player Input
EvtPlayerInput.description:
Called when a player sends an updated input to the server.
Note: The input keys event value is the set of keys the player is currently pressing, not the keys that were pressed or released.

examples:
on sheep eat:
	kill event-entity
	broadcast "A sheep stole some grass!"
on falling block land:
	event-entity is a falling dirt
	cancel event
EvtEndermanSheepSilverfishFallingBlock.name: On Enderman/Sheep/Silverfish/Falling Block
EvtEndermanSheepSilverfishFallingBlock.description:
Called when an enderman places or picks up a block, a sheep eats grass, a silverfish boops into/out of a block or a falling block lands and turns into a block respectively.
event-block represents the old block and event-blockdata represents the new replacement that'll be applied to the block.

examples:
on anvil prepare:
	event-item is set # result item
	chance of 5%:
		set repair cost to repair cost * 50%
		send "You're LUCKY! You got 50% discount." to player
EvtAnvilPrepare.name: On Anvil Prepare
EvtAnvilPrepare.description: Called when an item is put in a slot for repair by an anvil. Please note that this event is called multiple times in a single item slot move.

examples:
on load:
	set {running::%script%} to true
on unload:
	set {running::%script%} to false
EvtScriptLoadUnload.name: On Script Load/Unload
EvtScriptLoadUnload.description:
Called directly after the trigger is loaded, or directly before the whole script is unloaded.
The keyword 'async' indicates the trigger can be ran asynchronously,

examples:
on broadcast:
	set broadcast-message to "&c[BROADCAST] %broadcasted message%"
EvtBroadcast.name: On Broadcast
EvtBroadcast.description: Called when a message is broadcasted.

examples:
on vault display item:
	set event-item to a netherite ingot
EvtVaultDisplayItem.name: On Vault Display Item
EvtVaultDisplayItem.description: Called when a vault in a trial chamber is about to display an item.

EvtLanguageChange.name: On Language Change
EvtLanguageChange.description: Called after a player changed their language in the game settings. You can use the language expression to get the current language of the player.

examples:
on attack attempt:
    if event is cancelled:
        broadcast "%attacker% failed to attack %victim%!"
    else:
        broadcast "%attacker% damaged %victim%!"
on attempt to attack an animal:
    cancel event
on attempting to attack an entity:
    if victim is a creeper:
        cancel event
on attempt to attack a zombie or creeper:
    attacker isn't holding a diamond sword
    cancel event
EvtAttemptAttack.name: On Attempt Attack
EvtAttemptAttack.description:
Called when a player attempts to attack an entity.
The event will be cancelled as soon as it is fired for non-living entities.
Cancelling this event will prevent the attack and any sounds from being played when attacking.
Any damage events will not be called if this is cancelled.

examples:
on item damage:
	cancel event
EvtItemDamage.name: On Item Damage
EvtItemDamage.description: Called when an item is damaged. Most tools are damaged by using them; armor is damaged when the wearer takes damage.

examples:
every 2 seconds:
every minecraft hour:
every tick: # can cause lag depending on the code inside the event
every minecraft days:
EvtPeriodical.name: Periodical
EvtPeriodical.description: An event that is called periodically.

EvtDrop.name: On Drop
EvtDrop.description: Called when a player drops an item from their inventory, or an entity drops an item, such as a chicken laying an egg.

examples:
on death:
on death of player:
on death of a wither or ender dragon:
	broadcast "A %entity% has been slain in %world%!"
EvtDeath.name: On Death
EvtDeath.description: Called when a living entity (including players) dies.

examples:
on smelting start:
	if the smelting item is raw iron:
		set total cook time to 1 second
on smelting start of raw iron:
	add 20 seconds to total cook time
EvtStartSmelt.name: On Start Smelt
EvtStartSmelt.description: Called when a furnace starts smelting an item in its ore slot.

examples:
on horse jump:
	push event-entity upwards at speed 2
EvtHorseJump.name: On Horse Jump

examples:
on send command list:
	set command list to command list where [input does not contain ":"]
	remove "help" from command list
EvtSendCommandList.name: On Send Command List
EvtSendCommandList.description: Called when the server sends a list of commands to the player. This usually happens on join. The sent commands can be modified via the sent commands expression. Modifications will affect what commands show up for the player to tab complete. They will not affect what commands the player can actually run. Adding new commands to the list is illegal behavior and will be ignored.

EvtFurnaceItemExtract.name: On Furnace Item Extract
EvtFurnaceItemExtract.description: Called when a player takes any item out of the furnace.

examples: on crop growth:
EvtBlockGrowth.name: On Block Growth
EvtBlockGrowth.description: Called when a crop grows. Alternative to new form of generic grow event.

examples: on form of snow:
EvtForm.name: On Form
EvtForm.description: Called when a block is created, but not by a player, e.g. snow forms due to snowfall, water freezes in cold biomes. This isn't called when block spreads (mushroom growth, water physics etc.), as it has its own event (see spread event).

examples:
on resource pack request response:
	if the resource pack was declined or failed to download:
on resource pack deny:
	kick the player due to "You have to install the resource pack to play in this server!"
EvtResourcePackRequestResponse.name: On Resource Pack Request Response
EvtResourcePackRequestResponse.description: Called when a player takes action on a resource pack request sent via the send resource pack effect. The resource pack condition can be used to check the resource pack state. This event will be triggered once when the player accepts or declines the resource pack request, and once when the resource pack is successfully installed or failed to download.

examples:
on dismount:
	kill event-entity
EvtEntityDismount.name: On Entity Dismount

examples: on world init of "world_the_end":
EvtWorldInit.name: On World Init
EvtWorldInit.description:
Called when a world is initialized. As all default worlds are initialized before
any scripts are loaded, this event is only called for newly created worlds.
World management plugins might change the behaviour of this event though.

examples:
on enchant:
	if the clicked button is 1: # offer 1
		set the applied enchantments to sharpness 10 and unbreaking 10
EvtEnchant.name: On Enchant
EvtEnchant.description: Called when a player successfully enchants an item. To get the enchanted item, see the enchant item expression

examples: on level change:
EvtLevelChange.name: On Level Change
EvtLevelChange.description: Called when a player's level changes, e.g. by gathering experience or by enchanting something.

examples:
on player move:
	if player does not have permission "player.can.move":
		cancel event
on skeleton move:
	if event-entity is not in world "world":
		kill event-entity
on player turning around:
	send action bar "You are currently turning your head around!" to player
EvtMoveRotate.name: On Move / Rotate
EvtMoveRotate.description:
Called when a player or entity moves or rotates their head.
NOTE: Move event will only be called when the entity/player moves position, keyword 'turn around' is for orientation (ie: looking around), and the combined syntax listens for both.
NOTE: These events can be performance heavy as they are called quite often.

EvtIgnition.name: On Ignition
EvtIgnition.description: Called when a block starts burning, i.e. a fire block is placed next to it and this block is flammable. The burn event will be called when the block is about do be destroyed by the fire.

examples:
on player stop using item:
	broadcast "%player% used %event-item% for %event-timespan%."
EvtStopUsingItem.name: On Stop Using Item
EvtStopUsingItem.description:
Called when a player stops using an item. For example, when the player releases the interact button when holding a bow, an edible item, or a spyglass.
Note that event-timespan will return the time the item was used for.

examples: on area cloud effect:
EvtAoeCloudEffect.name: On AoE Cloud Effect
EvtAoeCloudEffect.description: Called when area effect cloud applies its potion effect. This happens every 5 ticks by default.

examples: on tame:
EvtTame.name: On Tame
EvtTame.description: Called when a player tames a wolf or ocelot. Can be cancelled to prevent the entity from being tamed.

examples:
on player picking a diamond block:
	cancel event
	send "You cannot pick diamond blocks!" to the player
EvtPlayerPickItem.name: On Player Pick Item
EvtPlayerPickItem.description:
Called when a player picks an item, block or an entity using the pick block key (default middle mouse button).
The past event-slot represents the slot containing the item that will be put into the players hotbar, or nothing, if the item is not in the inventory.
The event-slot represents the slot in the hotbar where the picked item will be placed.
Both event-slots may be set to new slots.

examples:
on burn:
on burn of oak wood, oak fences, or chests:
on burn of oak_log[axis=y]:
EvtBurn.name: On Burn
EvtBurn.description: Called when a block is destroyed by fire.

examples:
on loot generate:
	chance of 10%
	add 64 diamonds to the loot
	send "You hit the jackpot at %event-location%!"
EvtLootGenerate.name: On Loot Generate
EvtLootGenerate.description:
Called when a loot table of an inventory is generated in the world.
For example, when opening a shipwreck chest.

examples:
on vehicle collision:
on vehicle collision with obsidian:
on vehicle collision with a zombie:
EvtVehicleCollision.name: On Vehicle Collision
EvtVehicleCollision.description: Called when a vehicle collides with a block or entity.

examples: on chunk unload:
EvtChunkUnload.name: On Chunk Unload
EvtChunkUnload.description: Called when a chunk is unloaded due to not being near any player.

examples: on explosion prime:
EvtExplosionPrime.name: On Explosion Prime
EvtExplosionPrime.description: Called when an explosive is primed, i.e. an entity will explode shortly. Creepers can abort the explosion if the player gets too far away, while TNT will explode for sure after a short time.

examples:
on brew:
	broadcast event-item
on brewing of speed potion:
on brew finished for speed 2 potion:
EvtBrewingComplete.name: On Brewing Complete
EvtBrewingComplete.description: Called when a brewing stand finishes brewing an ingredient and changes the potions.

examples: on tool break:
EvtItemBreak.name: On Item Break
EvtItemBreak.description:
Called when a player breaks their tool because its damage reached the maximum value.
This event cannot be cancelled.

examples: on respawn:
EvtRespawn.name: On Respawn
EvtRespawn.description: Called when a player respawns via death or entering the end portal in the end. You should prefer this event over the death event as the player is technically alive when this event is called.

EvtEntityJump.name: On Entity Jump

examples:
on level progress change:
	set {_xp} to event-experience
	broadcast "%{_xp}%"
EvtExperienceChange.name: On Experience Change
EvtExperienceChange.description: Called when a player's experience changes.

examples:
on connect:
	player doesn't have permission "VIP"
	number of players is greater than 15
	kick the player due to "The last 5 slots are reserved for VIP players."
EvtConnect.name: On Connect
EvtConnect.description: Called when the player connects to the server. This event is called before the player actually joins the server, so if you want to prevent players from joining you should prefer this event over on join.

examples:
on player trade:
	chance of 50%:
		cancel event
		send "The trade was somehow denied!" to player
EvtPlayerTrade.name: On Player Trade
EvtPlayerTrade.description: Called when a player has traded with a villager.

examples:
on spawn of a zombie:
on spawn of an ender dragon:
	broadcast "A dragon has been sighted in %world%!"
EvtSpawn.name: On Spawn
EvtSpawn.description: Called when an entity spawns (excluding players).

examples:
on redstone change:
	send "someone is using redstone" to console
EvtRedstone.name: On Redstone
EvtRedstone.description: Called when the redstone current of a block changes. This event is of not much use yet.

examples:
on vehicle move:
	broadcast past event-location
	broadcast event-location
EvtVehicleMove.name: On Vehicle Move
EvtVehicleMove.description:
Called when a vehicle moves.
Please note that using this event can cause lag if there are multiple vehicle entities, i.e. Horse, Pig, Boat, Minecart

examples:
on xp spawn:
	world is "minigame_world"
	cancel event
EvtExperienceSpawn.name: On Experience Spawn
EvtExperienceSpawn.description:
Called whenever experience is about to spawn.
Please note that this event will not fire for xp orbs spawned by plugins (including Skript) with Bukkit.

examples:
on armor change:
	broadcast the old armor item
on helmet change:
EvtArmorChange.name: On Armor Change
EvtArmorChange.description: Called when armor pieces of a player are changed.

examples: on consume:
EvtConsume.name: On Consume
EvtConsume.description: Called when a player is done eating/drinking something, e.g. an apple, bread, meat, milk or a potion.

examples: on leaves decay:
EvtLeavesDecay.name: On Leaves Decay
EvtLeavesDecay.description: Called when a leaf block decays due to not being connected to a tree.

examples:
on worldborder bounds finish change:
	broadcast "Over the past %event-timespan%, the diameter of %event-worldborder% went from %past event-number% to %event-number%"
EvtWorldBorderBoundsFinishChange.name: On World Border Bounds Finish Change
EvtWorldBorderBoundsFinishChange.description:
Called when a moving world border has finished its move.
This event does not get called for virtual borders.

examples:
on brewing start:
	set the brewing time to 1 second
EvtBrewingStart.name: On Brewing Start
EvtBrewingStart.description: Called when a brewing stand starts brewing.

examples:
on world load of "world_nether":
	broadcast "The world %event-world% has been loaded!"
EvtWorldLoad.name: On World Load
EvtWorldLoad.description: Called when a world is loaded. As with the world init event, this event will not be called for the server's default world(s).

examples:
on spawn change:
	broadcast "someone changed the spawn!"
EvtSpawnChange.name: On Spawn Change
EvtSpawnChange.description: Called when the spawn point of a world changes.

examples: on book sign:
EvtBookSign.name: On Book Sign
EvtBookSign.description: Called when a player signs a book.

examples: on player leaving a bed:
EvtBedLeave.name: On Bed Leave
EvtBedLeave.description: Called when a player leaves a bed.
